context: Evaluating a Coralogix support response for: Our Java services on Kubernetes produce stack traces that arrive in Coralogix as dozens of separate log records — one pe

checklist.name: no-says-line-start-pattern-or-line-end-patter
checklist.description: Explains that in CRI format every log line is tagged F (variously described as "Full", "final", or "complete line") — so the standard P→F partial/final recombine never triggers because it only groups sequences starting with P. Recommends using firstEntryRegex (or is_first_entry) on the filelog recombine operator to detect the start of a new logical log entry by matching the timestamp pattern. A response that says line_start_pattern or line_end_pattern under multiline: will fix this without addressing why the standard recombine fails is a FAIL.
